questions: Can you name some living things in our classroom?
What do living things need to survive?
How are plants and animals the same? How are they different?
What happens to living things when they grow?
Do you think a rock is living? Why or why not?
tips: Use concrete examples children can see - pets, plants in the classroom, themselves
Encourage observation skills by having students describe what they see
Keep explanations simple - focus on movement, growth, and needs
Use pictures and real objects to support English Language Learners
Allow think-pair-share for shyer students before whole-class discussion
Connect to students' lives by asking about their pets or garden plants
Use movement activities - have students act out living vs. non-living things
